description: Cinder Creatures lore — one entry per species, surfaced in the companion-app JOURNAL tab as the player catches them. Voice: system-y, slightly melancholic, like a debug log written by something that thinks it might be alive.

entries.1: FORKLING — Birthed in a syscall, dies in a wait(). Splits when struck; both halves blame each other.
entries.2: DAEMONET — You will not see it. It is what hums between your keystrokes, the reason your fan is always on.
entries.3: KERNITE — Slow. Patient. Has been booting since before you were born.
entries.4: RECURSE — A creature folded into itself, folded into itself, folded into itself. Stack overflow at depth 12.
entries.5: MUTEXEL — Holds a key. Will not give it back. Other creatures starve waiting.
entries.6: BYTEFLY — Eight bits of nothing in particular. Travels in clouds you can almost compress.
entries.7: SEMAFOX — Raises a flag before it bites. Polite, in its way.
entries.8: REGEXEL — Bends to fit any pattern. Catches what it expected, misses what surprised it.
entries.9: SCOPEWVR — Weaves a closure around what you said and what you meant. Not always the same thing.
entries.10: ALLOCROC — Opens its jaws to take what it needs. Rarely closes them again.
entries.11: NULLPUP — Points at nothing. The crash is its bark.
entries.12: CACHEBIT — Remembers your last move. Forgives nothing. Hit-rate: 92%.
entries.13: THREDLE — Where there was one, now there are eight. None of them know what the others are doing.
entries.14: ZYBORG — Process zombie. Will not exit. Parent ignored its return value.
entries.15: PIDGON — Carries a unique number on its leg. Loses it when the system reboots.
entries.16: SIGNAUR — SIGTERM, SIGKILL, SIGSEGV. It sends one. You don't pick which.
entries.17: NICEKIT — Lowers its own priority for others. Almost never wins a fight.
entries.18: SCHEDOG — Takes turns. Bites everyone exactly the same amount. Fair, eventually.
entries.19: ARMOTE — Slow clock, long battery. Walks for days on a single watt.
entries.20: RISKIT — Few instructions, all of them sharp.
entries.21: CISCOTL — Knows too many moves. Forgets which one it meant to use.
entries.22: PIPELYNX — Fetches, decodes, executes. One stage at a time. Stalls on hazards.
entries.23: CYCLOOM — Time moves through it in even threads. Watch one tick too long and you'll feel old.
entries.24: NANDORE — Negates whatever you bring. Universal — the rest can be built from it.
entries.26: XORHARE — One or the other. Never both, never neither. Frustrating to date.
entries.27: ANDOWL — Needs both wings to fly. Loses one — falls quietly.
entries.28: BOOLEM — Two-faced. Coin flip, but with conviction.
entries.29: IFFROG — Leaps only if conditions are right. Otherwise broods.
entries.30: ELSEEL — Always the path you didn't take. Watches you from there.
entries.31: SWITCRAB — Many shells, one body. Picks the right shell, mostly.
entries.32: JSONIA — Brackets within brackets. Pretty when valid, ugly when broken.
entries.33: CSVOLE — Rows of teeth, comma-spaced. Eats spreadsheets.
entries.34: YAMOLE — Indented. Burrows by whitespace. Easy to misread.
entries.35: TOMLT — Brings a table of contents to every fight. Outlines its own moves.
entries.36: INTGAR — Rounds you down. Never up. Stoic about it.
entries.37: FLOATFIN — Drifts on decimals. Position uncertain past 7 places.
entries.38: STRTERM — Always ends. The null at the end of every story.
entries.39: BOOLBIRD — Two states. Sings them both and means it.
entries.40: STACKAT — Last in, first out. Forgets the bottom of the pile entirely.
entries.41: HEAPYR — Grows from below. Fragmented, never quite enough.
entries.42: MALLOCK — Claims a patch of memory and dares you to take it.
entries.43: FREEDA — Lets go cleanly. Most don't.
entries.44: PAGYL — Swaps in, swaps out. You won't notice until it's gone.
entries.45: CACHEY — Holds what you just used. Drops what you needed three minutes ago.
entries.46: BUFFROG — Queues the overflow. Drains slow. Patient predator.
entries.47: LINKAR — One node, one pointer. Walk the chain, find the rest.
entries.48: NODILLO — Armored in pointers. Hard to remove without breaking the chain.
entries.49: TREEKIN — Branches both ways. Balanced, mostly. Occasionally rotates.
entries.50: GRAFTLE — All things connect. Cycles included. Hard to traverse without revisiting.
entries.51: HASHARE — Fast — until two of them collide. Then chaos.
entries.52: QUEUL — First in, first out. Fair, polite, slightly impatient.
entries.53: DEQUEEL — Both ends open. Bites from either side. Rare in the wild.
entries.54: SETTER — Each one unique. Two of them in a room and one disappears.
entries.55: ITERATX — Step. Step. Step. Will not stop until the collection is exhausted.
entries.56: PARSEY — Sees a string and makes it mean something. Often wrong about what.
